duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Cross-chain replay of UUPS upgradeToAndCall via canSkipChainIdValidation whitelist allows wallet takeover or bricking on secondary chains where implementation address contains different or no code
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six threats describe the same vulnerability: upgradeToAndCall.selector whitelisted in canSkipChainIdValidation enables cross-chain replay of implementation upgrades. ECON-H002 selected for most comprehensive attack economics and clear exploit scenario.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: RIP-7212 precompile spoofing at hardcoded address 0x100 allows forged WebAuthn/passkey signatures on chains where a non-precompile contract exists at that address
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five threats describe the same vulnerability: staticcall to hardcoded VERIFIER=0x100 with only ret.length>0 and decode==1 check, no validation it is the actual RIP-7212 precompile. TECH-H001 selected for most detailed technical analysis of the code path and preconditions. Note: TECH2-H006 here refers to the first occurrence with title 'RIP-7212 Precompile Fallback Can Be Spoofed'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: removeOwnerAtIndex has no minimum owner count enforcement, allowing removal of all owners and permanent wallet bricking with no recovery path
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the same missing safety check: removeOwnerAtIndex deletes owner without verifying remaining owner count, enabling permanent fund lock. TECH-H003 selected for most detailed code-level analysis with specific line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicSpend validatePaymasterUserOp performs per-UserOp balance check (address(this).balance >= withdrawAmount) without cumulative accounting, allowing multiple UserOps to over-commit the contract's ETH balance in the same bundle
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All ten threats describe the same root cause: per-UserOp balance check in validatePaymasterUserOp without cumulative reservation, leading to over-commitment of _withdrawableETH across bundled UserOps. ECON-H006 (same-sender accumulation), ECON-H014 (deposit drain angle), and OPS2-H008 (future deposit drain) are specific impact manifestations of the same code defect. ECON-H001 selected for most comprehensive CRITICAL-rated analysis with detailed economics. Note: TECH2-H004 refers to first occurrence 'Multiple UserOps Balance Check Bypass'; TECH2-H003 refers to second occurrence 'cumulative solvency break'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: CoinbaseSmartWallet.initialize() with empty owners array leaves nextOwnerIndex at 0, defeating the re-initialization guard and allowing anyone to call initialize() again with their own owners
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six threats describe the same code defect: _initializeOwners with empty array is a no-op, nextOwnerIndex stays 0, re-initialization guard (nextOwnerIndex() != 0) never triggers. ECON-H004 selected for CRITICAL rating with economics and clear precondition analysis. Note: TECH2-H004 here refers to the second occurrence with title 'CoinbaseSmartWallet can be initialized multiple times'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicSpend postOp assert(mode != PostOpMode.postOpReverted) creates unrecoverable failure mode where gas refund is permanently lost if ETH transfer fails
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the same vulnerability: postOp assert on postOpReverted mode combined with delete-before-transfer pattern causes permanent gas refund loss. ECON-H005 selected for HIGH rating and best impact analysis. Note: TECH2-H005 here refers to first occurrence 'Unspent Gas Refund is Permanently Lost'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicSpend nonce permanently consumed in _validateRequest before signature validity and expiry are enforced, enabling nonce-burning griefing
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five threats describe the same ordering defect: _validateRequest sets _nonceUsed=true before signature/expiry validation, burning nonces for invalid or expired requests. OPS2-H007 selected for HIGH rating and most detailed attack description including paymaster path exploitation. Note: TECH2-H005 here refers to second occurrence 'nonce-burning grief vector'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Cross-chain replay of removeOwnerAtIndex with divergent owner indices across chains removes unintended owners, causing access loss or wallet bricking
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same vulnerability: removeOwnerAtIndex whitelisted for cross-chain replay, but index-based removal on chains with divergent owner state removes wrong owner. TECH-H010 selected for HIGH rating and comprehensive analysis combining index divergence with bricking risk.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: executeBatch iterates over unbounded user-controlled array with external calls, enabling gas-based DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same vulnerability: unbounded calls.length in executeBatch with no max limit causes out-of-gas failures. TECH-H016 selected for more detailed analysis. Note: TECH2-H006 here refers to second occurrence 'executeBatch gas-based DoS'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: execute/executeBatch perform arbitrary external calls with no reentrancy guard while msg.sender==address(this) is treated as authorized owner, enabling privilege escalation via callback or self-call
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same root mechanism: unrestricted external call in execute + self-call authorization in _checkOwner enables reentrancy-based or self-call-based privilege chaining. TECH-H015 selected for HIGH rating and most comprehensive technical description. Note: TECH2-H007 here refers to second occurrence 'execute reentrancy'; OPS2-H010 focuses on off-chain monitoring bypass aspect of same mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: WebAuthn.verify called with requireUV:false disables User Verification check, reducing passkey security to single-factor (physical presence only)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same vulnerability: _validateSignature hardcodes requireUV:false, skipping biometric/PIN verification for passkey signatures. ECON2-H003 selected for HIGH rating with detailed economics and physical access attack scenario. Note: TECH2-H007 here refers to first occurrence 'Passkey Authentication Bypasses UV'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicSpend.entryPointDeposit uses raw ETH transfer (safeTransferETH) to EntryPoint instead of calling depositTo(), potentially failing to credit the paymaster's deposit balance
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same code defect: entryPointDeposit sends ETH via safeTransferETH rather than calling IEntryPoint.depositTo, which may not properly credit the paymaster deposit. OPS-H013 selected for more detailed operational context and line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicSpend.withdraw() allows arbitrary ERC20 withdrawals (not restricted to ETH like the paymaster path), enabling drain of accidentally held tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same asymmetry: validatePaymasterUserOp enforces asset==address(0) but withdraw() accepts any asset, allowing ERC20 withdrawals if the contract holds tokens. OPS-H012 selected for HIGH rating and more detailed analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Non-canonical P-256 public key coordinates (x+p, y) pass FCL.ecAff_isOnCurve but encode to different bytes, enabling persistent owner backdoor surviving removal via MultiOwnable's byte-level deduplication
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe vulnerabilities stemming from lack of canonical normalization in owner registration. TECH-H017 describes the general encoding ambiguity concern; ECON2-H006 provides the specific P-256 non-canonical point exploit; ECON2-H007 extends to ERC-1271 consequences. ECON2-H006 selected for CRITICAL rating with concrete exploit path and detailed economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Cross-chain replay of owner addition operations grants unintended global access to chain-specific delegates via selective or automatic replay
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same vulnerability: owner additions whitelisted for cross-chain replay can be selectively replayed to grant persistent unauthorized access on other chains. OPS2-H001 selected for CRITICAL rating and more comprehensive analysis of selective replay attack.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: CoinbaseSmartWalletFactory.createAccount with msg.value for already-deployed accounts can lock or misdirect ETH
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same issue with createAccount forwarding msg.value via CREATE2 when the account already exists. ECON2-H008 selected for more detailed analysis of the permanent ETH lock scenario.
